metadata.title: StreetLens
metadata.description: A field instrument for the street. Open-source, portable audits of sidewalk accessibility, drainage, shade, and bike infrastructure. Now measuring Escazú, Costa Rica.

demoBanner.message: Demo data over real street geometry. Field pilot begins August 2026: no number shown is a real measurement.

map.ariaLabel: Interactive map of audited street segments in Escazú
map.zoomHint: Hold ⌘ or Ctrl and scroll to zoom

panel.heroStat: {pct}% of audited segments fail Ley 7600 accessibility minimums
panel.heroDemoNote: Demo figure. Not a real measurement.
panel.expand: Show full pilot stats and legend

detail.demoNote: Demo audit. Real field data replaces this corridor by corridor from August 2026.
detail.communityNote: Community-contributed. Not yet field-verified, so it carries no audit scores.
detail.cvNote: What a camera reading proposed and an admin approved. This is not a field audit, so it carries no audit scores.
detail.cvAssessmentNote: Written by the camera model, in English. Not a field audit.

login.title: Admin access
login.subtitle: Enter the shared admin password to review contributions.
login.errorRateLimited: Too many attempts. Try again in {seconds}s.
login.errorGeneric: Something went wrong. Please try again.

dashboard.title: Overview
dashboard.subtitle: Live figures from the current dataset.
dashboard.demoNote: Demo dataset over real OSM geometry. No figure is a real measurement until field collection begins.

queue.title: Verification queue
queue.empty: The queue is clear. New contributions will appear here.
queue.reasonPlaceholder: Why are you approving or rejecting this?
queue.errorGeneric: Could not save the review. Please try again.
queue.localNote: No database configured. Reviews are staged locally for a post-database apply step.
queue.captureUnreadable: This submission can no longer be read. Its session may have been deleted.

import.title: Bulk import
import.subtitle: Validate field geometry, then import. Dry-run first: nothing is written until you confirm.
import.fileHint: A GeoJSON FeatureCollection of LineStrings, or a CSV with name, highway, and coordinates columns.
import.verifiedHint: Only for field-team data. A verified import needs an auditor name.
import.emptyState: Choose a file and run validation to see a preview. Nothing is imported yet.
import.commitSuccess: Imported {count} features into the community dataset.
import.noValid: No valid features to import. Fix the errors above and re-validate.
import.auditorRequired: Enter an auditor name to import as verified.
import.errorParse: Could not read the file. Upload a GeoJSON FeatureCollection of LineStrings or a CSV.
import.errorGeneric: Something went wrong. Please try again.
import.localNote: No database configured. Imports are written to the local community dataset.

capture.title: Review a camera walk
capture.overbudget: Budget stopped before every frame was read
capture.noSegments: This submission produced no usable readings. Nothing here can reach the map, so rejecting it is the honest close.
capture.reasonPlaceholder: Why are you approving or rejecting this submission?
capture.errorGeneric: That did not save. Please try again.
capture.fixtureNote: No database is configured, so this submission is fixture data. Decisions are recorded locally.
capture.segmentDroppedNote: Every supporting frame was excluded or deleted, so this segment cannot be approved.
capture.assessmentNote: A model summary of the session. Your chosen scores are the numbers that publish.
capture.noAssessment: No assessment available for this segment.
capture.inspectorHint: Select a frame to see and correct its readings.
capture.mapLabel: Map of the session: GPS track, frame positions, and matched streets
capture.frameDeletedNote: This frame was deleted for privacy. Its image is gone; only the record that it existed remains.
capture.deleteConfirm: This permanently removes the frame's image.
capture.deleteError: That frame could not be deleted. Please try again.

choose.title: Help map Escazú
choose.subtitle: Add a street we missed, or flag a correction on one we mapped. A reviewer checks every submission before anything changes.
choose.addSegmentHint: Trace the sidewalk on the map
choose.proposeUpdateHint: Pick a mapped segment and tell us what's off
choose.recordWalkHint: Capture the street with your camera as you walk it

trace.title: Trace the street
trace.hint: Click to drop points along the sidewalk. Double-click, or press Finish, when you reach the end.
trace.points: {count, plural, =0 {No points yet} one {# point} other {# points}}
trace.needMore: Add at least two points to continue.

followStreets.label: Follow streets
followStreets.warning: Couldn't follow streets for part of this path. Those spans are drawn dashed.

select.title: Pick a segment
select.hint: Click any mapped street to propose a correction to it.

form.conditionsHint: Answer what you can. Skip anything you're unsure about.
form.notePlaceholder: A short note for the reviewer.
form.reasonPlaceholder: Tell the reviewer what's wrong or out of date.
form.contactPlaceholder: Email, only if you'd like a reply
form.contactHint: Never published. Used only to follow up on your submission.
form.photoSoon: Photo upload arrives with the field pilot. For now, describe it in your note.
form.pathPoints: {count, plural, one {Path traced · # point} other {Path traced · # points}}
form.honestNote: Submissions land as pending proposals. Nothing on the public map changes until a reviewer approves it.

success.title: Submitted for review
success.body: Thank you. Your proposal is pending review. A reviewer will check it before anything on the map changes.

errors.generic: Something went wrong. Please try again.
errors.rateLimited: You've sent a few submissions quickly. Please wait a moment and try again.
errors.invalid: Please check the highlighted fields and try again.
errors.network: Couldn't reach the server. Check your connection and try again.

meta.title: StreetLens: every street can be measured
meta.description: Every street can be measured. Most never are. StreetLens is an open-source field instrument that scores sidewalk accessibility, drainage, shade, and bike infrastructure, segment by segment. Now measuring Escazú, Costa Rica, on demo data until fieldwork begins in August 2026.
meta.ogTitle: Every street can be measured. Most never are.
meta.ogDescription: An open-source field instrument for the street: accessibility, drainage, shade, and bike infrastructure, scored segment by segment. Now measuring Escazú, Costa Rica.

hero.question: How good are our streets?
hero.subtitle: Cleaner, more livable cities for everyone start with streets you can measure.
hero.requestCity: Want this for your city? Request it

segments.title: Lowest-scoring streets
segments.caveat: Demo scores over real geometry. Not real measurements.

stats.demoFootnote: Fail rate and averages are demo figures over real OSM geometry.

map.affordance: Drag to pan. Hold ⌘ or Ctrl to zoom. Tap a segment to open it in the platform.

hero.openSource: The code is public: view / contribute

mission.heading: Cleaner, more livable cities for all.
mission.lead: We want cities that are cleaner, calmer, and more livable for everyone who moves through them. That does not begin with a bigger plan. It begins with an honest look at the street.

body.p1: Street quality is not just pavement. It is where infrastructure, daily life, and community intersect, and its condition quietly decides who a city serves and who it leaves behind.
body.p2: StreetLens is the instrument for that. The method is open and the same rubric applies in any city, so anyone can read a street the same way and check the result. Today the pilot runs on demo data over real geometry, and fieldwork in Escazú begins in August 2026.

measure.label: Measure what matters
measure.desc: Accessibility, drainage, shade, and bike infrastructure, scored segment by segment. A city that goes unmeasured cannot be made fair.

implement.label: Implement, don't just propose
implement.desc: We would rather measure one real street than publish another plan. Every reading is built to be concrete and actionable, and the data will grow into a public record other cities can learn from.

together.label: Grassroots and systemic
together.desc: Residents can score the streets they walk every day, while open data gives policymakers a shared, checkable picture. That is how better, cleaner, more livable cities get built for everyone.

measure.heading: Four lenses on every street.
measure.lead: Walkability breaks down into parts you can measure. Every segment is scored on four independent rubrics, the same way in any city. Two of them are climate resilience: shade tracks heat exposure, and drainage tracks flood risk. Each ramp is colorblind-safe and paired with a line-width channel, and every legend shows its bins.
measure.plateAlt: {name} scores across the pilot street network

accessibility.name: Accessibility
accessibility.desc: Curb ramps, tactile paving, and safe crossings. Whether everyone can actually use the sidewalk.

drainage.name: Drainage
drainage.desc: Grates, cross slope, and ponding history. From flood-prone to well-drained.

shade.name: Shade
shade.desc: Tree canopy and cover, and the heat they hold off. From exposed pavement to full canopy.

bike.name: Bike
bike.desc: Bike lanes and separation from traffic. From none to protected.

gap.heading: Accountability begins with a number.
gap.lead: You cannot budget, fix, or answer for what you never measured. A complete street starts as a measured one, and most cities cannot say which sidewalks fail, or where.
gap.body: Accessibility, drainage, and shade are almost never measured segment by segment, so they never enter a budget or a work order.<ley>Ley 7600, Costa Rica's equal-opportunity law, has required accessible sidewalks since 1996. No municipality yet publishes a compliance map.</ley> The cost of not knowing is not abstract. It is counted in people.<cosevi>COSEVI, the national road-safety council, publishes Costa Rica's annual road-safety counts.</cosevi>
gap.stat1Label: pedestrians run over in Costa Rica in 2024
gap.stat2Label: years of Ley 7600, still with no municipal compliance map
gap.stat3Label: of audited segments fail the accessibility minimum
gap.stat3Note: Demo figure over real geometry. Not a measurement.

pilot.heading: Escazú first. Then every corridor that will have us.
pilot.lead: Every instrument needs a first street. Ours is San Antonio de Escazú, mapped segment by segment over real geometry. Field collection begins in August 2026, corridor by corridor.

figure.label: Figure 3
figure.claim: The pilot corridor, mapped in full.
figure.support: San Antonio de Escazú over real OpenStreetMap geometry. The rest of the canton follows, on the same rubric.
figure.source: Source: StreetLens over OpenStreetMap geometry. Demo scores until fieldwork begins August 2026.
figure.alt: Rendered map of the San Antonio de Escazú pilot corridor

pilot.beginning: This is a pilot, and a beginning.
pilot.demoNote: Demo audit over real geometry. No score shown is a measurement until fieldwork begins in August 2026.

method.heading: We walk the street, then we score it.
method.lead: Each segment is audited against an established rubric. Nothing is inferred from a desk.
method.body: An auditor walks the segment, records each item on site, and the score is computed from those observations.<maps>MAPS-Mini is a validated microscale audit of pedestrian streetscapes: sidewalks, crossings, and buffers.</maps> The same instrument runs on any street network, and every number traces back to something a person saw and wrote down.<lanamme>LANAMME-UCR is Costa Rica's national road-materials laboratory at the University of Costa Rica, the source for pavement and condition standards.</lanamme>

maps.title: MAPS-Mini
maps.desc: A validated microscale audit of pedestrian streetscapes: sidewalks, crossings, and buffers.

lanamme.title: LANAMME-UCR
lanamme.desc: Costa Rica's national road-materials laboratory at the University of Costa Rica, for pavement and condition standards.

ley.title: Ley 7600
ley.desc: Costa Rica's equal-opportunity law, which sets the accessibility minimums a sidewalk must meet.

field.title: Field audit
field.desc: Every score traces to an on-site observation and, in time, a photo.

method.anatomyCaption: Example segment. Demo audit over real OSM geometry, not a measurement.

collect.heading: Two ways in, one reviewed dataset.

field.title: Field audits
field.desc: A trained auditor walks a corridor and records the rubric on site. This is the source of truth.

community.title: Community reports
community.desc: Anyone can add a street we missed or flag a correction from the map. Every submission is reviewed before it changes anything public.

pipeline.heading: Every audit trains the next one.
pipeline.body: Field audits are the source of truth, and they stay that way. From the August 2026 pilot on, every corridor an auditor walks is also photographed, and those images become labeled training data. Over time they will train a computer-vision and machine-learning pipeline that reads sidewalk condition and obstructions straight from a photo, so every future audit scores faster than the last. No model scores a street today. When one does, a person still verifies what it reports.

crossSection.label: Figure 4
crossSection.claim: What the four lenses actually read.
crossSection.support: A street's right-of-way in section, with the sidewalk, buffer, and carriageway each measured against the rubric above.
crossSection.source: Source: StreetLens drawing over Reglamento a la Ley 7600 (Decreto 26831-MP) sidewalk minimums.
crossSection.alt: Engineering cross-section of a street right-of-way, annotating the sidewalk, buffer, and carriageway with the four StreetLens lenses and the Ley 7600 minimum dimensions.

anatomy.label: Figure 5
anatomy.claim: How one segment becomes four scores.
anatomy.support: The same segment in plan, with the audit rubric, score bins, width channel, and community casing that resolve to the readout above.
anatomy.source: Source: StreetLens scoring schematic. Width bins per Reglamento a la Ley 7600 (Decreto 26831-MP).
anatomy.alt: Plan-view diagram of a street segment showing the audit rubric, score bins, width measurement channel, and community-report casing that produce the segment's four lens scores.

pipeline.label: Figure 6
pipeline.claim: Field audits today. A verified model tomorrow.
pipeline.support: Every walked corridor is photographed, and those images become training data for a computer-vision pipeline that a person always checks.
pipeline.source: Roadmap, not built. The machine-learning path drawn dashed does not score any street today.
pipeline.alt: Method pipeline diagram: field audits flow to open data today, with a dashed roadmap loop where a computer-vision and machine-learning model scores streets and a human always verifies the result.

grounding.heading: Open data, or it didn't happen.
grounding.lead: StreetLens is not a black box. It leans on established rubrics and open datasets, every score ships with its formula, and the code is public.

lanamme.name: LANAMME-UCR
lanamme.note: National road-materials laboratory, University of Costa Rica.

ley.name: Ley 7600
ley.note: Equal Opportunities for Persons with Disabilities Act.

cosevi.name: COSEVI
cosevi.note: National Road Safety Council road-safety data.

openSource.label: Open source
openSource.note: The whole platform is public on GitHub: the code, the rubric, and the scoring method, open for anyone to read, run, or fork.

openData.label: Open data
openData.note: Every segment exports with its geometry and its score formula.

roadmap.heading: From one corridor to an open standard.
roadmap.lead: The pilot is step one. The instrument is built to scale, from a single corridor to a whole canton, across cantons, and out to anyone who needs the data.

pilot.title: Pilot corridor
pilot.desc: San Antonio de Escazú, mapped over real geometry. Field collection begins August 2026.

canton.title: Full canton
canton.desc: Every corridor in Escazú, audited on the same rubric.

compare.title: Canton comparison
compare.desc: Neighboring cantons measured the same way, so results compare directly.

api.title: Open API and data
api.desc: Governments and researchers query the scored network directly.

real.a: Not yet. Every score you see today is demo data generated over real OpenStreetMap geometry, so the map behaves exactly as it will with field data. Real measurements replace it corridor by corridor once fieldwork begins in August 2026.

scores.a: Each layer is scored 0 to 100 from a rubric of observed items, where higher is better. The map pairs a colorblind-safe color ramp with a line-width channel, and the legend always shows the value bins. Every score ships with its formula.

ai.q: Does StreetLens use machine learning or computer vision?
ai.a: Not to score streets today. Every score you see is demo data, and real scores will come from auditors in the field. Their photos become the training data for a computer-vision and machine-learning pipeline that will speed up future audits, and a person will always verify what it produces. Machine learning and computer vision are on the roadmap, not in today's numbers.

city.a: That is the goal. The rubric, the app, and the data format are open and portable, built to run on any street network. Today only the Escazú pilot is live. If you want to bring StreetLens to your city, reach out through the project on GitHub.

fieldwork.a: Field collection in San Antonio de Escazú is planned for August 2026. Until then the pilot runs on demo data over the real corridor.

contribute.a: Yes. You can add a street we missed or flag a correction from the map. A reviewer checks every submission before anything on the public map changes.

who.a: StreetLens is a project of CUSP, the Collective for Urbanism and Sustainable Progress, led by Gianluca Fonseca. It is open source and built in the open.

cta.heading: Measure what the city forgot to.
cta.support: Open the live map, or add a street you know. Every score ships with its formula.

footer.tagline: An open field instrument for the street. Now measuring Escazú, Costa Rica.
footer.openData: Street geometry from OpenStreetMap, under ODbL 1.0. Scores are demo data until field collection begins in August 2026.
footer.built: A project of CUSP, the Collective for Urbanism and Sustainable Progress.

meta.title: Record a walk | StreetLens
meta.description: Record a street with your phone as you walk it. StreetLens stamps every frame with GPS and matches it to a segment, so sidewalk condition gets measured where nobody has measured it.

start.title: Walk a street. Record what is actually there.
start.lead: Your phone keeps a frame every few metres and stamps it with GPS. A reviewer checks the session before any of it becomes public data.
start.how1: Portrait. Camera pointed forward down the sidewalk, not at the ground.
start.how2: Walk at a normal pace. A frame is kept about once a second and every six metres.
start.how3: Keep this screen in front of you. The camera stops the moment you switch apps.
start.askCameraHint: Frames of the street ahead. Nothing leaves your phone until you review it.
start.askLocationHint: Stamps each frame so it can be matched to a street.
start.privacy: Frames become public data after review. Do not record people close up, and do not record inside private property.

hud.gpsWarn: Weak GPS. Fixes above {meters} m get dropped when your session is matched.
hud.gpsDenied: Location is off, so frames cannot be placed and none are being kept.
hud.gpsUnavailable: No GPS signal. This happens indoors and under heavy cover.
hud.wakeFailed: Your screen may sleep. Keep it awake yourself if you can.
hud.notDurable: This browser cannot save frames to disk. Do not reload or switch apps, or the session is lost.

paused.title: Recording stopped
paused.body: The camera cannot run while this page is in the background. Everything up to now is saved. Resuming starts a new segment, because we will not pretend the gap did not happen.

review.title: Your session
review.droppedLead: Frames the recorder chose not to keep, and why.
review.capFrames: Recording stopped on its own at the {count} frame limit.
review.capDuration: Recording stopped on its own at the {minutes} minute limit.
review.storageFull: Recording stopped because storage filled up. The session up to that point is safe.
review.contactHint: Only used if a reviewer needs to ask you something about this submission.
review.consent: Uploading means these frames become public data once a reviewer approves them.
review.discardConfirm: Discard this submission? The frames are deleted from this device and cannot be recovered.
review.tooShort: A walk needs at least one frame and two GPS fixes before it can be uploaded.

uploadError.backend_not_live_title: Uploads are not switched on yet
uploadError.backend_not_live_body: Your session is saved on this device and nothing was lost. The part of the pipeline that receives walks is still being built. Come back later and it will still be here.
uploadError.storage_not_configured_body: This build cannot reach the frame store. Your session is saved on this device.
uploadError.offline_body: Your frames are safe on this device. Retry once you are back online.
uploadError.rate_limited_body: This device uploaded recently. Your session is saved. Try again later.
uploadError.rejected_title: The server would not take this submission
uploadError.rejected_body: Your session is saved on this device. This usually means it fell outside the mapped area.
uploadError.unknown_body: Your frames are safe on this device. You can retry.

done.title: Your session is in
done.body: It is queued for matching and review. Frames become public data once a reviewer approves them.

recover.title: You have a walk that was never uploaded
recover.body: {frames} frames over {distance}, still saved on this device.

unsupported.title: This browser cannot record a walk
unsupported.insecure_context: Recording needs a secure connection. Browsers only hand the camera to pages served over https, and this page was not.
unsupported.no_camera_api: This browser does not give web pages a camera. Try Safari on iPhone, or Chrome on Android.
unsupported.no_geolocation: This browser does not give web pages your location, so frames could not be matched to a street.

camera.denied_body: Recording needs the camera. Allow it for this site in your browser settings, then try again.
camera.not_found_body: This device did not offer a camera facing away from you.
camera.in_use_body: Another app or tab has it. Close that one, then try again.
camera.insecure_context_body: Browsers only hand the camera to pages served over https.
camera.unsupported_body: This browser does not give web pages a camera.
camera.unknown_body: Try again. If it keeps failing, reload the page.

drops.cadence: Too soon after the last one
drops.displacement: Phone had not moved far enough
drops.duplicate: Same picture as the frame before

chooser.title: Two ways to hand us a street.
chooser.lead: Record as you walk, or upload a video you already shot. Both end up as the same reviewed, public data.
chooser.liveBody: Your phone keeps a frame every few metres and stamps it with GPS as you go. There is nothing to supply afterwards.
chooser.uploadTitle: Upload a video you already have
chooser.uploadBody: This device pulls the frames out of the file. The video carries no GPS, so you supply the route afterwards.

videoStart.title: Upload a walk you already recorded.
videoStart.lead: Pick a video of a street. This device reads the frames out of it, and then you tell us where it went.
videoStart.routeTitle: The route step is required, every time.
videoStart.routeBody: A phone video carries no GPS track. Browsers hand a web page the pixels and nothing else, so there is nothing for us to read and nothing to detect. Without a route the frames cannot be matched to a street, so you upload a GPX or draw the line you walked.
videoStart.how1: Pick the file. It stays on this device and nothing is sent yet.
videoStart.how2: This browser reads out about one frame a second. A long video gets sampled more sparsely.
videoStart.how3: You supply the route, then you review everything before any of it is uploaded.
videoStart.drop: Drop a video here, or choose a file.
videoStart.dropHint: Any video this browser can play. It is read here, on this device.
videoStart.back: Back to the two ways in
videoStart.privacy: Frames become public data after review. Do not upload people close up, and do not upload footage from inside private property.

videoExtract.title: Pulling the frames out
videoExtract.probing: Reading the file and working out how long it is.
videoExtract.lead: This runs on this device. Nothing has been sent anywhere.
videoExtract.keepOpen: Keep this screen open. Every frame is saved as it is read, so a reload picks up where it stopped rather than starting over.
videoExtract.sparserTitle: This video is long, so the frames are sampled more sparsely.
videoExtract.sparserBody: One frame every {seconds} seconds instead of every second. The whole street is still covered end to end. Keeping one a second would hit the frame cap and cover only the first stretch of your session.
videoExtract.storageFullBody: This device ran out of room, so reading stopped. The frames read so far are still here. Free some space and pick the file again, or discard this video.

videoRoute.title: Tell us where this video went.
videoRoute.lead: The frames are out. They have no place yet: the video carries no GPS track, so the route has to come from you.
videoRoute.gpxBody: A track exported from a watch, a bike computer, or a planning tool. If it carries timestamps we keep them, because measured times beat an assumed pace.
videoRoute.gpxTimed: This file times every point, so those times are used as they are.
videoRoute.gpxUntimed: This file has no times, so the video's own length is spread evenly along the line. That assumes you kept a steady pace.
videoRoute.traceTitle: Or draw the line you walked
videoRoute.traceBody: Click along the streets you walked. The router follows the streets between your points, and says so when it cannot.
videoRoute.traceHint: A drawn route needs at least two points.
videoRoute.chosen_trace: The drawn line will be used.
videoRoute.mapLabel: Route map. Click the map to drop points along the route you walked.
videoRoute.points: {count, plural, =0 {No points} one {# point} other {# points}}
videoRoute.fallbackBody: We could not find streets between two of your points, so that stretch is drawn as a dashed straight line. It is a guess, not a routed street. Move the points onto the road to fix it.

gpxError.not_gpx_title: That is not a GPX file
gpxError.not_gpx_body: There is no GPX in this file. Export the track as GPX from whatever recorded it, then pick that file.
gpxError.no_trackpoints_body: The file reads, but it has no track points in it. A file of waypoints or a plain area will not do: we need the line that was actually travelled.
gpxError.malformed_coordinates_title: A coordinate in this file is broken
gpxError.malformed_coordinates_body: One track point has a latitude or longitude we cannot read. We refuse the whole file rather than skip the point, because a skipped point does not announce itself: it just quietly cuts a corner.
gpxError.unterminated_trackpoint_body: The file ends in the middle of a track point, so it was truncated while it was being written. Export it again.
gpxError.unknown_title: This GPX could not be read
gpxError.unknown_body: The file did not parse. Export the track again, or draw the route on the map instead.

videoError.video_too_short_title: This video is too short to sample
videoError.video_too_short_body: There is not enough of it to keep a single frame from. Upload a walk of at least a few seconds.
videoError.no_frames_extracted_title: No frames came out of this video
videoError.no_frames_extracted_body: The file was read end to end and produced nothing usable. Try a different export of it, or record the street with the live recorder instead.
videoError.extract_failed_body: The decoder stopped part way through and we cannot say the frames are complete. Try the file again, or try a different export of it.
videoError.no_canvas_title: This browser would not give us a canvas
videoError.no_canvas_body: Frames are drawn on a canvas before they are saved, and this browser refused one. Try Safari on iPhone, or Chrome on Android or desktop.
videoError.no_video_element_title: This browser cannot open the file
videoError.no_video_element_body: It would not create the video element the fallback reader needs. Try Safari on iPhone, or Chrome on Android or desktop.
videoError.video_load_failed_title: This browser cannot play this video
videoError.video_load_failed_body: The file did not load at all, which usually means the format is one this browser does not handle. Try an MP4 exported from the camera app.
videoError.video_load_failed_timeout_title: This video took too long to load
videoError.video_load_failed_timeout_body: The browser never finished opening the file. Try it again, and if it keeps stalling try a smaller export.
videoError.unknown_duration_title: We cannot tell how long this video is
videoError.unknown_duration_body: The file reports no duration, so there is no way to plan where to sample it. Re-export it, or try a file from the camera app itself.
videoError.seek_failed_title: The browser could not move through the video
videoError.seek_failed_body: Seeking to a sample point failed part way through. Try the file again, or try a different export of it.
videoError.seek_failed_timeout_body: The browser stopped responding to seeks. This happens on very large files. Try a smaller export of the same walk.
videoError.seek_stuck_title: The browser stopped moving through the video
videoError.seek_stuck_body: It kept returning the same frame instead of the next sample point, so the rest would have been duplicates. Try a different export of the file.
videoError.unsupported_title: This browser cannot decode this video
videoError.unsupported_body: Neither reader would take this track. Try Chrome on desktop or Android, which decode the widest range of files.
videoError.parse_failed_title: This file could not be read as a video
videoError.parse_failed_body: Its container did not parse. It may be partly downloaded, or it may not be a video at all.
videoError.no_moov_body: The index a player needs to find the frames is missing, which is what a file looks like when a recording was interrupted or a transfer stopped early. Copy it again from the phone that shot it.
videoError.no_video_track_title: There is no video in this file
videoError.no_video_track_body: The file opened, but it holds no video track. An audio-only file has nothing to show a street with.
videoError.unknown_title: This video could not be read
videoError.unknown_body: Something in the file stopped the reader and we cannot say what. Try a different export of it, or record the street with the live recorder instead.

videoReview.title: Your video
videoReview.source_trace: A line you drew on the map.
videoReview.timed: The GPX times its own points, so the frames are placed against those times.
videoReview.derived: The route has no times of its own, so the video's length was spread evenly along it. That assumes you kept a steady pace.
videoReview.storageFull: Reading stopped because storage filled up. The frames read up to that point are safe.
videoReview.notDurable: This browser cannot save frames to disk. Do not reload or switch apps before this is uploaded.
videoReview.contactHint: Only used if a reviewer needs to ask you something about this video.
videoReview.consent: Uploading means these frames become public data once a reviewer approves them.
videoReview.discardConfirm: Discard this video? The frames read out of it are deleted from this device and cannot be recovered.
videoReview.tooShort: An upload needs at least one frame and a route of at least two points.

clock.title: Line the video up with the GPX.
clock.body: We guessed the start from the file's own timestamp, and that guess is often wrong. Drag until the preview frame sits where you remember shooting it.
clock.label: Start time correction, in seconds
clock.previewBody: The first frame we kept, and where this start time puts it on the route.
clock.outsideTitle: This start puts the frame off the route
clock.outsideBody: At this offset the first frame falls outside the times the GPX covers, so it would have nowhere to sit. Drag back until it lands on the line.
clock.hiddenTitle: There is nothing to line up here
clock.hiddenBody: This route has no clock of its own, so its times come from the video's start. Moving the start moves the frames and the route together, and every frame lands exactly where it did before. A slider here would change a number and change nothing about the data.

videoDone.title: Your video is in
videoDone.body: It is queued for matching and review. Frames become public data once a reviewer approves them.

meta.title: Walk status | StreetLens
meta.description: The processing status of a walk recorded with StreetLens.

status.title: Your session
status.body: This page updates itself while your session is processed. A person reviews every walk before any of it reaches the map, so none of this is instant.

state.pending_upload: Waiting for the frames to arrive.
state.matching: Matching your frames to the streets you walked.
state.extracting: Reading your frames. This is the slow part, and it moves faster while this page is open.
state.cost_paused: Reading is paused on this submission.
state.review_ready: Your session is waiting for a reviewer.
state.approved: Approved. What your session measured is public data now.
state.rejected: A reviewer did not approve this submission, so none of it became public data.
state.failed: This submission could not be processed.

status.framesFailed: Frames that could not be read: {count}. The rest of the session still counts.

paused.title: Reading is paused
paused.body: This submission reached its processing budget, so nothing more is being spent on it automatically. Somebody has to look at it and decide. Your frames are safe in the meantime.

failedNotice.title: This submission could not be processed
failedNotice.body: Something in matching or reading went wrong, and we would rather say so than show you a number we do not trust. Nothing from this submission became public data.

status.segmentsLead: Street segments matched so far: {count}. The scores stay provisional until a reviewer approves the session.
status.waitNote: You can close this page. The session keeps processing either way, and this link brings you back to it.

not_found.title: No walk at this link
not_found.body: This link does not point at a walk we hold. Make sure you copied all of it, including the part after the last slash.

invalid_session.title: This is not a walk link
invalid_session.body: The id in this address is not the right shape for a walk. Make sure you copied the whole link.

unavailable.title: Status is unreachable
unavailable.body: The service that tracks walks is not answering right now. Your session is unaffected. This page keeps trying.

server_error.title: Could not read the status
server_error.body: Something went wrong on our side reading this submission. Your frames are unaffected. This page keeps trying.

network.title: No connection
network.body: This page cannot reach StreetLens. Your session keeps processing without it. This page keeps trying.
